methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rhoman Aerospace is a seed-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector founded by Thomas Youmans and Thomas Callen. The valuation universe for this sector comprises 8 private companies, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comparable companies exist in this narrow subsector, limiting direct market-based reference points.

The peer-median approach is not applicable to this valuation. While 5 same-sector peers exist within Drones & Autonomous, none meet the threshold criteria for robust triangulation. Specifically, zero peers operate at the same seed stage, zero at adjacent stages, and zero at similar valuation tiers. Attempting to apply all-sector medians would conflate companies at vastly different development and funding stages, introducing material distortion to the analysis.

Given these constraints, conventional quantitative methods cannot be reliably applied. The analysis does not support a specific valuation range based on the available data. Key uncertainties include the absence of stage-appropriate and tier-appropriate comparables, which prevents anchoring to market precedent. Any valuation determination would require either: (1) expansion of the analytical aperture to include earlier-stage funding events within the broader autonomous vehicle ecosystem, (2) qualitative assessment of Rhoman's specific technology differentiation relative to the listed peers, or (3) alternative methods such as cost-to-develop or risk-adjusted DCF, none of which are supported by the current dataset.
